models.name: Yi-34B
models.note: Flagship open bilingual base model that briefly topped Hugging Face's Open LLM Leaderboard.

models.name: Yi-6B
models.note: Small bilingual (EN/ZH) base model from 01.AI's debut Yi series; Apache-2.0 weights.

models.name: Yi-1.5 34B
models.note: Upgraded Yi flagship trained on 500B extra tokens; stronger coding, math and reasoning.

models.name: Yi-1.5 9B
models.note: Popular mid-size Yi-1.5 variant balancing quality and deployability.

models.name: Yi-Lightning
models.note: API-only MoE flagship; reached #6 on LMArena at launch, first Chinese model to pass GPT-4o there.

models.name: AI21 Jurassic-2 (Ultra)
models.note: AI21's second-gen flagship (Ultra/Mid/Light) with instruction-following and multilingual support; API-only.

models.name: AI21 Jamba
models.note: First production-grade hybrid SSM (Mamba)-Transformer MoE; 12B active of 52B total, 256K context, Apache 2.0.

models.name: AI21 Jamba 1.5 Large
models.note: Flagship open hybrid SSM-Transformer MoE, 94B active of 398B total; among the longest-context open models.

models.name: AI21 Jamba 1.5 Mini
models.note: Open hybrid SSM-Transformer, 12B active of 52B total; 256K context under the Jamba Open Model License.

models.name: Luminous Supreme (70B)
models.note: Germany's first large-scale sovereign LLM; API-only 70B flagship of the Luminous family.

models.name: Pharia-1-LLM-7B (control)
models.note: Open, EU-compliance-focused 7B; weights public under the non-commercial Open Aleph License.

models.name: Qwen-VL
models.note: First Qwen vision-language model (built on Qwen-7B); accepts image, text and bounding-box inputs.

models.name: Qwen-7B
models.note: First open Qwen release; 3T-token base under the bespoke Tongyi-Qianwen License, launched the family.

models.name: Qwen-14B
models.note: Mid-size Gen-1 dense model, followed the 7B two months later.

models.name: Qwen-1.8B
models.note: Smallest Gen-1 model, aimed at low-latency and edge deployment.

models.name: Qwen-72B
models.note: Gen-1 flagship trained on 3T tokens; beat Llama2-70B and topped GPT-3.5 on several benchmarks.

models.name: Qwen1.5-72B
models.note: Gen-1.5 flagship; added Group Query Attention and a 32K context across all sizes.

models.name: Qwen1.5-MoE-A2.7B
models.note: First Qwen Mixture-of-Experts model; 2.7B active params match a dense 7B at ~25% training cost.

models.name: CodeQwen1.5-7B
models.note: First Qwen code specialist; 3T code tokens, 64K context for long-code tasks.

models.name: Qwen1.5-110B
models.note: Largest dense Qwen at the time; later powered the closed Qwen-Max-0428.

models.name: Qwen-Max-0428
models.note: Early proprietary Qwen-Max flagship built on Qwen1.5-110B; launched the closed DashScope track.

models.name: Qwen2-57B-A14B
models.note: Gen-2 MoE (14B active) with ~30B-dense-class quality; Apache 2.0.

models.name: Qwen2-72B
models.note: Gen-2 flagship (7T tokens, 128K context); topped the Hugging Face Open LLM Leaderboard on release.

models.name: Qwen2-7B
models.note: Widely-used small Gen-2 dense model; Apache 2.0, 128K context on Instruct.

models.name: Qwen2-Math-72B
models.note: First Qwen math specialist; Instruct variant outperformed GPT-4o and Claude 3.5 on math.

models.name: Qwen2.5-Math-72B
models.note: Leading open math LLM with tool-integrated reasoning (also 1.5B/7B).

models.name: Qwen2-VL-72B
models.note: Gen-2 vision-language flagship with dynamic-resolution and video understanding (2B/7B shipped Aug 30).

models.name: Qwen2.5-14B
models.note: Apache-2.0 Gen-2.5 dense model, 128K context.

models.name: Qwen2.5-32B
models.note: Popular Apache-2.0 mid-size Gen-2.5 dense model, strong quality-per-cost.

models.name: Qwen2.5-72B
models.note: Gen-2.5 flagship trained on 18T tokens; big MMLU/MATH/coding gains over Qwen2.

models.name: Qwen2.5-7B
models.note: Highly-downloaded small Gen-2.5 dense model; Apache 2.0.

models.name: Qwen2.5-Coder-32B
models.note: Coding flagship rivaling GPT-4o on code; Apache 2.0 (family also 0.5B-14B).

models.name: QwQ-32B-Preview
models.note: First Qwen reasoning model, o1-style chain-of-thought; Apache 2.0 experimental preview.

models.name: QVQ-72B-Preview
models.note: Experimental visual-reasoning model built on Qwen2-VL-72B for step-by-step multimodal reasoning.

models.name: Qwen2.5-Max
models.note: Large proprietary MoE served via API; competitive with DeepSeek V3 and GPT-4o.

models.name: Qwen2.5-VL-72B
models.note: Gen-2.5 vision flagship with agentic GUI grounding and long-video understanding (3B/7B/32B/72B).

models.name: QwQ-32B
models.note: RL-trained reasoning flagship matching DeepSeek-R1 at a fraction of the size; Apache 2.0.

models.name: Qwen3-14B
models.note: Mid-size Gen-3 dense model with thinking mode; Apache 2.0.

models.name: Qwen3-235B-A22B
models.note: Gen-3 flagship MoE (22B active) with hybrid thinking/non-thinking modes; rivals R1, o1, Gemini 2.5 Pro.

models.name: Qwen3-30B-A3B
models.note: Efficient Gen-3 MoE (3B active) that outcompetes QwQ-32B; Apache 2.0.

models.name: Qwen3-32B
models.note: Largest Gen-3 dense model; hybrid-reasoning, Apache 2.0.

models.name: Qwen3-8B
models.note: Popular small Gen-3 dense model; hybrid reasoning, Apache 2.0.

models.name: Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507
models.note: July refresh dropping hybrid mode for a dedicated non-thinking Instruct model; 256K context.

models.name: Qwen3-Coder-480B-A35B
models.note: Most powerful open agentic coding MoE (35B active, 256K-1M context), comparable to Claude Sonnet 4.

models.name: Qwen3-Max
models.note: Biggest Qwen yet, over 1T params (exact undisclosed) trained on 36T tokens; API-only preview.

models.name: Tülu 2+DPO 70B
models.note: Llama-2 fine-tune and the largest publicly released DPO-trained model at the time; pivotal demonstration that the DPO post-training recipe scales to 70B.

models.name: OLMo 7B
models.note: Ai2's first Open Language Model — a truly open LLM released with weights, full pretraining data (Dolma), training code and checkpoints under permissive licenses.

models.name: OLMoE-1B-7B-0924
models.note: First 100% open sparse mixture-of-experts LLM: 6.9B total / ~1.3B active params, matching or beating larger dense models like Llama2-13B-Chat.

models.name: Molmo 72B
models.note: Flagship open vision-language model (built on Qwen2-72B); pioneered image 'pointing' and rivals GPT-4o, Claude 3.5 and Gemini 1.5 on academic benchmarks with 1000x less data.

models.name: Tülu 3 70B
models.note: Open post-training flagship on Llama 3.1; introduced the RLVR (Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards) recipe and surpassed Llama 3.1 70B Instruct.

models.name: OLMo 2 13B
models.note: Flagship of the Nov 2024 OLMo 2 launch; best fully open model of its size, with the Instruct variant beating Qwen 2.5 14B Instruct.

models.name: OLMo 2 7B
models.note: Fully open 7B trained on up to 5T tokens; outperforms Llama 3.1 8B on English academic benchmarks despite lower training FLOPs.

models.name: Tülu 3 405B
models.note: Scaled the fully open Tülu 3 RLVR recipe to 405B (on Llama 3.1); matches or beats DeepSeek V3 and GPT-4o on key benchmarks.

models.name: OLMo 2 32B
models.note: First fully open model to outperform GPT-3.5 Turbo and GPT-4o mini; trained to 6T tokens and post-trained with Tülu 3.1.

models.name: OLMo 3 7B
models.note: Lighter-weight member of the OLMo 3 family (Base/Think/Instruct) built on the Dolma 3 / Dolci stack; runs on a wider range of hardware.

models.name: OLMo 3-Think 32B
models.note: Latest flagship and strongest fully open reasoning model; narrows the gap to Qwen 3-32B-Thinking on reasoning benchmarks while trained on ~6x fewer tokens.

models.name: Molmo 2 8B
models.note: Latest open multimodal flagship (Qwen3-based); extends Molmo's grounded pointing to video with frame-level spatial/temporal grounding and multi-object tracking.

models.name: Amazon Titan Text (Express & Lite)
models.note: Amazon's first-generation in-house text foundation models on Bedrock, predecessors to Nova.

models.name: Amazon Nova Lite
models.note: Low-cost multimodal (text/image/video in) model with a 300K-token context, tuned for speed.

models.name: Amazon Nova Micro
models.note: Text-only, lowest-cost/lowest-latency Nova tier with a 128K-token context, on Amazon Bedrock.

models.name: Amazon Nova Pro
models.note: Highest-accuracy multimodal Nova at launch (300K context); the workhorse mid-tier before Premier.

models.name: Amazon Nova Premier
models.note: Amazon's most capable Nova: 1M-token context for complex agentic/long-doc tasks and distillation teacher.

models.name: Claude 1
models.note: Anthropic's first public model; brought Constitutional AI training to market.

models.name: Claude 2
models.note: First broadly available Claude with a 100K-token context window and stronger coding.

models.name: Claude Instant 1.2
models.note: Fast, low-cost model; the small/quick tier that seeded the later Haiku lineage.

models.name: Claude 2.1
models.note: Industry-first 200K context window, 2x fewer hallucinations, and early tool use.

models.name: Claude 3 Opus
models.note: Claude 3 flagship; first Claude to top GPT-4 on major public benchmarks.

models.name: Claude 3 Sonnet
models.note: Balanced mid-tier of the Claude 3 family; ~2x faster than Claude 2.

models.name: Claude 3 Haiku
models.note: Fastest, cheapest Claude 3; vision-capable with near-instant responses.

models.name: Claude 3.5 Sonnet
models.note: Mid-tier that beat Opus 3; launched the Artifacts feature and led coding.

models.name: Claude 3.5 Haiku
models.note: Announced Oct 2024 (API GA early Nov); fast tier matching Opus 3 on many coding tasks.

models.name: Claude 3.7 Sonnet
models.note: Anthropic's first hybrid-reasoning model with an extended-thinking mode; shipped Claude Code.

models.name: Claude Opus 4
models.note: Opened the Claude 4 generation; took the SWE-bench Verified coding lead.

models.name: Claude Sonnet 4
models.note: Balanced Claude 4 model; strong coding at far lower cost than Opus 4.

models.name: Claude Opus 4.1
models.note: Incremental Opus 4 upgrade with improved agentic coding and reasoning.

models.name: Claude Sonnet 4.5
models.note: Best coding/agent Sonnet at release, with long-horizon autonomy gains.

models.name: Claude Haiku 4.5
models.note: First Claude-4-generation Haiku; near-frontier quality at a fraction of the cost.

models.name: Claude Opus 4.5
models.note: Efficiency-focused flagship with a major price cut and large output-token reductions.

models.name: Claude Opus 4.6
models.note: Flagship bringing 1M-token context to general availability plus multi-agent features.

models.name: Apple OpenELM (270M–3B)
models.note: Fully open, reproducible on-device family (270M/450M/1.1B/3B) with code, logs, and public-data training.

models.name: Apple Foundation Model (on-device, ~3B)
models.note: ~3B on-device model powering Apple Intelligence; beat Phi-3-mini/Mistral-7B/Gemma-7B in Apple's evals.

models.name: Apple DCLM-Baseline 7B
models.note: Data-centric research model from the DataComp-LM benchmark; open weights + dataset, ~2.5T tokens.

models.name: Baichuan2-13B
models.note: Flagship of the Baichuan 2 open series; a pivotal early Chinese open bilingual model with a full technical report.

models.name: Baichuan2-7B
models.note: Early strong bilingual (zh/en) open LLM trained on 2.6T tokens; Base + Chat released.

models.name: Baichuan 3
models.note: Trillion-parameter-class closed API model; strong on Chinese medical/reasoning tasks, params undisclosed.

models.name: Baichuan 4
models.note: Closed flagship launched with the Baixiaoying AI assistant; topped several domestic Chinese benchmarks at release.

models.name: ERNIE 3.0 Titan
models.note: 260B knowledge-enhanced model (with Peng Cheng Lab); then the largest Chinese dense LM and first knowledge-enhanced 100B+ model.

models.name: ERNIE Bot (ERNIE 3.5)
models.note: China's first public ChatGPT rival; the ERNIE Bot chatbot debuted on the ERNIE 3.5 model.

models.name: ERNIE 4.0
models.note: Baidu's GPT-4-class flagship, unveiled at Baidu World 2023; later refreshed as ERNIE 4.0 Turbo (2024).

models.name: ERNIE X1
models.note: Deep-thinking multimodal reasoning model positioned against DeepSeek R1 at roughly half the price.

models.name: ERNIE 4.5 300B-A47B
models.note: Flagship open-weights text MoE (300B total / 47B active) under Apache-2.0; anchor of a family scaling 0.3B to 424B.

models.name: ERNIE 4.5 VL 424B-A47B
models.note: Largest of the open ERNIE 4.5 release; multimodal (vision-language) MoE, 424B total / 47B active, Apache-2.0.

models.name: ERNIE 5.0 (Preview)
models.note: Natively omni-modal flagship jointly modeling text/image/audio/video; reported ~2.4T params, launched at Baidu World 2025.

models.name: SantaCoder 1.1B
models.note: BigCode's first model; 1.1B multilingual code LLM with infilling, proof-of-concept for The Stack and the RAIL-licensed open-code effort.

models.name: StarCoder (15.5B)
models.note: Pivotal open code LLM (15.5B, 8K context, multi-query attention) trained on The Stack across 80+ languages; matched Codex-cushman.

models.name: StarCoder2 15B
models.note: Flagship 2nd-gen BigCode model, NVIDIA-trained on The Stack v2 (600+ languages, 4T+ tokens); Hugging Face/ServiceNow/NVIDIA collaboration.

models.name: StarCoder2 3B
models.note: ServiceNow-trained 3B StarCoder2 for laptop/edge code generation; outperforms the original 15B StarCoder on many benchmarks.

models.name: BLOOM (176B)
models.note: First openly released 100B+ model; 46 natural + 13 programming languages, built by 1000+ researchers under the RAIL license.

models.name: BLOOM-7B1
models.note: 7.1B multilingual variant of BLOOM, the practical size for most downstream and fine-tuning use.

models.name: BLOOMZ (176B)
models.note: BLOOM fine-tuned on the xP3 crosslingual task mixture for zero-shot multilingual instruction following.

models.name: Doubao (1st-gen)
models.note: ByteDance's debut foundation-model family, launched at the Volcano Engine FORCE conference with aggressive pricing.

models.name: Doubao-1.5-Pro
models.note: Sparse MoE (~20B active) matching GPT-4o/Claude 3.5 Sonnet at far lower cost; adds a Deep Thinking mode.

models.name: Seed1.5-Thinking
models.note: RL-trained reasoning model (aka doubao-1.5-thinking-pro) with strong AIME/coding results; ~20B active MoE.

models.name: Doubao-Seed-1.6
models.note: Adaptive-CoT multimodal flagship with 256K context; ~63% cheaper than Doubao 1.5, spans thinking/flash variants.

models.name: Seed-OSS-36B
models.note: ByteDance's Apache-2.0 open release; 36B dense, native 512K context, tunable thinking budget.

models.name: Cerebras-GPT 13B
models.note: Largest of a seven-model Chinchilla-optimal, fully-open GPT family trained on CS-2 wafer-scale hardware.

models.name: BTLM-3B-8K
models.note: Bittensor Language Model: 3B with 7B-class quality and 8K context, trained on SlimPajama.

models.name: Dolphin 2.5 Mixtral 8x7B
models.note: Flagship uncensored Dolphin: QLoRA fine-tune of Mixtral 8x7B (Apache-2.0) with refusals filtered out; a benchmark for the 'uncensored' MoE fine-tune wave.

models.name: Command
models.note: Cohere's first-generation flagship text-generation model, API-only; foundation of the enterprise Command line.

models.name: Command R
models.note: 35B RAG/tool-use model, 128K context; first open-weights (CC-BY-NC) Command release.

models.name: Command R7B
models.note: Smallest, fastest R-series model (~7-8B) for on-device/edge RAG and tool use (CC-BY-NC).

models.name: Command A
models.note: 111B enterprise flagship, 256K context, runs on two GPUs; rivals GPT-4o on agentic tasks (CC-BY-NC).

models.name: Aya 101
models.note: 13B mT5-based instruction model covering 101 languages; Apache-2.0, built by 3,000+ researchers.

models.name: Aya 23 35B
models.note: Command-based multilingual model (also 8B) focused on depth across 23 languages (CC-BY-NC).

models.name: Aya Expanse 32B
models.note: SOTA open multilingual model (also 8B), 128K context, 23 languages via data arbitrage + model merging (CC-BY-NC).

models.name: Aya Vision 32B
models.note: Cohere's first vision-language model (also 8B); multilingual multimodal across 23 languages (CC-BY-NC).

models.name: DBRX
models.note: Fine-grained MoE (132B total / 36B active, 16 experts, 4 active); a leading open model at launch, pretrained on 12T tokens.

models.name: DeciLM-7B
models.note: NAS-designed variable-GQA 7B; billed as the fastest and most accurate 7B base LLM at launch.

models.name: DeepSeek Coder 33B
models.note: DeepSeek's first public model; open code LLM (1.3B/6.7B/33B) trained 87% code, beat Codex/GPT-3.5 on code benchmarks.

models.name: DeepSeek LLM 67B
models.note: First general-purpose foundation model (dense, 7B/67B); 67B surpassed Llama-2 70B on code, math and reasoning.

models.name: DeepSeekMoE 16B
models.note: Introduced the DeepSeekMoE architecture (fine-grained + shared experts); 16.4B total, 2.8B active, matched Llama-2 7B at ~40% compute.

models.name: DeepSeekMath 7B
models.note: Math-specialist LLM that debuted GRPO reinforcement learning (later reused for R1); hit 51% on MATH near GPT-4 level at 7B.

models.name: DeepSeek-VL 7B
models.note: First DeepSeek vision-language model (1.3B/7B), dense hybrid vision encoder for real-world multimodal understanding.

models.name: DeepSeek-V2 236B
models.note: MoE flagship (236B total, 21B active) introducing Multi-head Latent Attention; aggressive pricing triggered China's LLM price war.

models.name: DeepSeek-Coder-V2 236B
models.note: MoE code model (236B/21B, also 16B Lite) reaching GPT-4-Turbo-level coding across 338 languages, 128K context.

models.name: DeepSeek-V2.5
models.note: Merged DeepSeek-V2-Chat and Coder-V2 into one 236B model with better alignment, writing and instruction-following.

models.name: DeepSeek-VL2
models.note: MoE vision-language family (Tiny/Small/base; ~1.0B/2.8B/4.5B active) with dynamic image tiling for advanced multimodal tasks.

models.name: DeepSeek-V3 671B
models.note: Frontier open MoE (671B total, 37B active) trained on 14.8T tokens for ~$5.6M; rivaled GPT-4o/Claude 3.5 at a fraction of the cost.

models.name: DeepSeek-R1 671B
models.note: Open reasoning flagship (671B/37B) matching OpenAI o1 on math/code; its launch triggered the largest single-day US market-cap drop.

models.name: DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-70B
models.note: Largest R1 distill; Llama-3.3-70B fine-tuned on R1 reasoning data, popular self-hostable strong reasoner (MIT).

models.name: DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B
models.note: Best of the R1 distilled dense models; Qwen2.5-32B fine-tuned on R1 traces, outperformed o1-mini for dense-model SOTA.

models.name: DeepSeek-R1-Zero
models.note: Reasoning model trained by pure large-scale RL with no supervised fine-tuning — first open proof that reasoning emerges from RL alone.

models.name: DeepSeek-V3-0324
models.note: March 2025 V3 checkpoint with big gains in reasoning, math (AIME 39.6→59.4), coding and front-end generation; MIT-licensed.

models.name: DeepSeek-Prover-V2 671B
models.note: Formal theorem-proving model (Lean 4) built on V3; 88.9% on MiniF2F-test, state-of-the-art in neural theorem proving.

models.name: DeepSeek-R1-0528
models.note: Major R1 refresh: deeper chain-of-thought (AIME 2024 79.8→91.4), fewer hallucinations, JSON/function calling; approached o3/Gemini 2.5 Pro.

models.name: DeepSeek-V3.1
models.note: Hybrid model unifying thinking and non-thinking modes in one 671B/37B MoE; stronger tool use and agent workflows, 128K context.

models.name: GPT-Neo 2.7B
models.note: First open GPT-3-style replication; largest freely available autoregressive LM at launch, trained on The Pile under MIT.

models.name: GPT-J-6B
models.note: 6B Pile-trained model (mesh-transformer-jax, Apache 2.0); the go-to open GPT-3 alternative for two years.

models.name: GPT-NeoX-20B
models.note: Largest publicly available dense LM at release; trained with the GPT-NeoX framework on The Pile, Apache 2.0.

models.name: Pythia-1.4B
models.note: Smaller Pythia checkpoint spanning the suite's controlled scaling ladder from 70M to 12B.

models.name: Pythia-12B
models.note: Flagship of the 8-model Pythia interpretability suite; identical data/order across scales with 154 released checkpoints.

models.name: Pythia-6.9B
models.note: Mid-large Pythia size widely used as a reproducible base for training-dynamics and scaling studies.

models.name: Llama-3-ELYZA-JP-8B
models.note: Llama-3 Japanese continual-pretrain; the flagship series claimed Japanese performance rivaling GPT-4.

models.name: Llama-3.1-ELYZA-JP-70B
models.note: Llama-3.1-based 70B Japanese flagship with longer context, reported to exceed GPT-4 on Japanese tasks.

models.name: LaMDA
models.note: Dialogue model behind early Bard; 137B.

models.name: Gopher
models.note: DeepMind's 280B dense scaling milestone.

models.name: Chinchilla
models.note: Compute-optimal scaling; 70B outperforms 280B Gopher.

models.name: PaLM (540B)
models.note: 540B Pathways model; chain-of-thought breakthrough.

models.name: UL2 20B
models.note: Open unified pretraining; strong chain-of-thought at 20B.

models.name: Flan-T5 XXL
models.note: Instruction-tuned T5; popularized the Flan recipe.

models.name: PaLM 2
models.note: Powered Bard in 2023; basis for Med-PaLM 2 and Codey.

models.name: Gemini 1.0 Nano
models.note: On-device Gemini for Pixel; smallest 1.0 tier.

models.name: Gemini 1.0 Pro
models.note: General-purpose 1.0 workhorse; powered Bard at launch.

models.name: Gemini 1.0 Ultra
models.note: Google's first frontier model; launched Gemini Advanced.

models.name: Gemini 1.5 Pro
models.note: Breakthrough long context up to 1M tokens (MoE).

models.name: Gemma 2B
models.note: Smallest first-gen open Gemma; runs on modest hardware.

models.name: Gemma 7B
models.note: First open-weights model built from Gemini research.

models.name: CodeGemma 7B
models.note: Gemma tuned for code completion and generation.

models.name: Gemini 1.5 Flash
models.note: Distilled fast tier; became the free Gemini model.

models.name: Gemma 2 27B
models.note: Flagship Gemma 2; rivals models more than twice its size.

models.name: Gemma 2 9B
models.note: Efficient mid-size Gemma 2 with grouped-query attention.

models.name: Gemini 1.5 Flash-8B
models.note: Smallest, cheapest 1.5 Flash for high-volume tasks.

models.name: Gemini 2.0 Flash
models.note: Agentic, multimodal workhorse with native tool use.

models.name: Gemini 2.0 Flash-Lite
models.note: First Flash-Lite; cost-optimized 2.0 tier.

models.name: Gemini 2.0 Pro Experimental
models.note: Strongest 2.0 coding/reasoning preview.

models.name: Gemma 3 12B
models.note: Sweet spot for 12GB consumer cards.

models.name: Gemma 3 27B
models.note: Best-in-class single-GPU open vision-language model of early 2025.

models.name: Gemma 3 4B
models.note: Compact multimodal Gemma 3 for edge deployment.

models.name: Gemini 2.5 Pro
models.note: First Gemini with always-on thinking; reasoning leader of 2025.

models.name: Gemini 2.5 Flash
models.note: High-throughput tier with controllable thinking budget.

models.name: Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite
models.note: Cheapest, fastest 2.5 tier; thinking optional.

models.name: Gemini 3 Pro
models.note: Generational leap over 2.5; agentic-coding frontier.

models.name: Gemini 3 Flash
models.note: Pro-grade reasoning at Flash speed and a quarter the cost.

models.name: Gemini 3.1 Pro
models.note: Coding-arena leader; extended long-context frontier.

models.name: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite
models.note: Ultra-cheap 3.x tier for high-volume workloads.

models.name: Gemma 4
models.note: Reasoning/agentic-focused open flagship; most capable Gemma yet.

models.name: Gemini 3.5 Flash
models.note: First Flash to beat the prior-generation Pro flagship; I/O 2026 headline.

models.name: H2O-Danube 1.8B
models.note: H2O.ai's debut small LLM, Llama-style architecture, tuned for efficient on-device inference.

models.name: H2O-Danube3 4B
models.note: Apache-2.0 small model trained on 6T tokens that can run locally on a modern smartphone.

models.name: IDEFICS 80B
models.note: First open-access reproduction of DeepMind's Flamingo; interleaved image-text vision-language model.

models.name: Zephyr 7B Beta
models.note: DPO-aligned Mistral-7B fine-tune that popularized distilled preference tuning; weights, recipe and data all open.

models.name: Idefics2 8B
models.note: Compact Apache-2.0 VLM with strong OCR, roughly 10x smaller than IDEFICS 80B.

models.name: SmolLM2 1.7B
models.note: Fully-open small-model family (135M/360M/1.7B) for on-device use, trained on curated data.

models.name: SmolLM3 3B
models.note: Open 3B multilingual dual-mode reasoner with 128K context, trained on 11T tokens.

models.name: Granite 34B Code Instruct
models.note: Largest Granite Code model, depth-upscaled from Granite-20B-Code; top of IBM's first open code-intelligence family.

models.name: Granite 8B Code Instruct
models.note: IBM's workhorse code model (family spans 3B-34B) trained on 116 languages; Apache 2.0 base + instruct variants for enterprise code tasks.

models.name: Granite 3.0 2B Instruct
models.note: Smaller dense Granite 3.0 model for cost-efficient enterprise inference; Apache 2.0, same 12T-token training corpus.

models.name: Granite 3.0 8B Instruct
models.note: Flagship 3rd-gen Granite enterprise LLM (dense 2B/8B + 1B/3B MoE), Apache 2.0, trained on 12T tokens for RAG, tool use and summarization.

models.name: Granite 3.1 8B Instruct
models.note: Granite 3.1 extends context from 4K to 128K via progressive training and improves benchmark performance; Apache 2.0.

models.name: Granite 3.2 8B Instruct
models.note: Adds toggleable chain-of-thought reasoning (on/off) plus a vision model; inference scaling lets it rival much larger models.

models.name: Granite 3.3 8B Instruct
models.note: Adds fill-in-the-middle code generation and stronger math/symbolic reasoning; shipped alongside IBM's first open speech-to-text model.

models.name: Granite 4.0 H Small
models.note: Flagship hybrid Mamba-2/Transformer MoE (32B total, 9B active) cutting memory ~70%; Apache 2.0, first open models with ISO 42001 certification.

models.name: iFlytek Spark V1.0 (Xinghuo)
models.note: iFlytek's first Spark cognitive large model, one of China's earliest ChatGPT challengers.

models.name: iFlytek Spark V3.0 (Xinghuo)
models.note: Third-gen Spark benchmarked against ChatGPT-3.5, unveiled at the 2023 iFlytek 1024 Developer Festival.

models.name: iFlytek Spark V4.0 (Xinghuo)
models.note: GPT-4-Turbo-class flagship trained on fully domestic compute; later refreshed as Spark 4.0 Turbo (Oct 2024).

models.name: iFlytek Spark X1 (Xinghuo X1)
models.note: iFlytek's first deep-reasoning model, notable for being trained entirely on domestic (Huawei) compute.

models.name: Jais-13B
models.note: First flagship open Arabic-English bilingual LLM; built with MBZUAI and Cerebras.

models.name: Jais 70B
models.note: Largest Jais Arabic-English model, launched alongside 20+ other Jais variants in 2024.

models.name: Inflection-1
models.note: Inflection's first in-house LLM powering the Pi chatbot; roughly GPT-3.5-class, ~72.7% MMLU.

models.name: Inflection-2
models.note: Flagship trained on ~5,000 H100s; beat Google's PaLM 2 on common benchmarks, billed as 2nd-most-capable LLM.

models.name: Inflection-2.5
models.note: GPT-4-class upgrade for Pi using ~40% of GPT-4's training compute; last major model before the Microsoft exodus.

models.name: InternLM2-20B
models.note: Flagship of the InternLM2 generation; 200K context, Apache-2.0 code, detailed technical report.

models.name: InternLM2.5-7B
models.note: Widely used open 7B with ~20% better reasoning than InternLM2 and near-perfect 1M-token needle retrieval.

models.name: InternLM3-8B-Instruct
models.note: Apache-2.0 hybrid model with deep-thinking + normal chat modes, trained on only 4T tokens (~75% cost saving).

models.name: Airoboros 70B (L2)
models.note: Flagship of the fully synthetic, GPT-4-generated self-instruct pipeline (family began May 2023); showed one person could build a competitive 70B on Llama 2.

models.name: Skywork-13B
models.note: Bilingual foundation model trained on 3.2T tokens; SOTA Chinese LM at release, freely commercial.

models.name: Skywork-MoE-Base
models.note: 146B/22B-active MoE (16 experts) using upcycling; first open large MoE runnable on a single 8x4090 server.

models.name: Skywork-o1-Open-Llama-3.1-8B
models.note: Early open o1-style reasoning model on Llama-3.1-8B with process reward models and slow-thinking traces.

models.name: Skywork-R1V (38B)
models.note: First open-sourced multimodal reasoning model with visual chain-of-thought; 69.0 MMMU, 67.5 MathVista.

models.name: LG EXAONE 3.0 7.8B Instruct
models.note: LG's first publicly released bilingual (Korean/English) open model.

models.name: LG EXAONE 3.5 32B Instruct
models.note: Instruction-tuned family released in 2.4B / 7.8B / 32B sizes; the 32B is the flagship.

models.name: LG EXAONE Deep 32B
models.note: Reasoning-specialized line (2.4B/7.8B/32B) targeting math, science and coding benchmarks.

models.name: LG EXAONE 4.0 32B
models.note: Marketed as Korea's first open-weight hybrid reasoning model, with a toggleable thinking mode.

models.name: Vicuna 13B (v1.1)
models.note: LLaMA fine-tuned on 70K ShareGPT conversations; claimed ~90% of ChatGPT quality and popularized GPT-4-as-judge evaluation.

models.name: Vicuna 13B v1.5
models.note: Llama 2-based refresh of Vicuna (also a 16K-context variant); the widely-used, more-permissive successor to the original LLaMA build.

models.name: OPT-175B
models.note: First GPT-3-scale (175B) model released with weights plus full training code and logbook to researchers.

models.name: OPT-66B
models.note: Largest freely downloadable OPT size, released alongside Meta's training logbook.

models.name: Galactica 120B
models.note: Science LLM trained on papers/code; public demo pulled after three days over hallucination concerns.

models.name: OPT-IML 30B
models.note: Instruction-tuned OPT fine-tuned on ~2,000 NLP tasks (OPT-IML Bench); early instruction-tuning at scale.

models.name: LLaMA 13B
models.note: LLaMA-1 13B; Meta reported it rivaling GPT-3 175B on many benchmarks.

models.name: LLaMA 33B
models.note: Mid-large LLaMA-1 (~32.5B), trained on 1.4T tokens.

models.name: LLaMA 65B
models.note: Flagship of Meta's first LLaMA release that sparked the modern open-weights LLM wave.

models.name: LLaMA 7B
models.note: Smallest first-gen LLaMA, trained on 1T tokens for efficient research use.

models.name: Llama 2 13B
models.note: Mid-size Llama 2 base model.

models.name: Llama 2 13B Chat
models.note: RLHF-tuned dialogue version of Llama 2 13B.

models.name: Llama 2 70B
models.note: First open-weights LLM licensed for commercial use; Llama 2 flagship base model.

models.name: Llama 2 70B Chat
models.note: Flagship RLHF chat model, fine-tuned on 1M+ human annotations for dialogue.

models.name: Llama 2 7B
models.note: Compact Llama 2 base model, 2T-token pretraining and 4K context.

models.name: Llama 2 7B Chat
models.note: RLHF-tuned dialogue version of Llama 2 7B.

models.name: Code Llama 13B
models.note: 13B coding model with fill-in-the-middle generation.

models.name: Code Llama 34B
models.note: 34B code flagship scoring ~53.7% on HumanEval at release.

models.name: Code Llama 34B Python
models.note: Python-specialized Code Llama, further trained on 100B tokens of Python code.

models.name: Code Llama 7B
models.note: Code-specialized Llama 2 with infilling and long-context support.

models.name: Code Llama 70B
models.note: Largest Code Llama, released Jan 2024 as the strongest of the coding family.

models.name: Code Llama 70B Instruct
models.note: Instruction-tuned 70B coder; among the top open models on HumanEval in early 2024.

models.name: Llama 3 70B
models.note: Llama 3 flagship, competitive with Gemini 1.5 Pro on MMLU and HumanEval.

models.name: Llama 3 8B
models.note: Efficient Llama 3, 15T-token pretraining, 8K context, best-in-tier at launch.

models.name: Llama 3.1 405B
models.note: First openly available model rivaling top closed frontier models across knowledge, math and tool use.

models.name: Llama 3.1 70B
models.note: 128K-context 70B for large-scale AI-native applications.

models.name: Llama 3.1 8B
models.note: Upgraded 8B with 128K context and eight-language multilingual support.

models.name: Llama 3.2 11B Vision
models.note: First Llama vision model, adding image understanding at 11B.

models.name: Llama 3.2 1B
models.note: On-device text model with 128K context for edge and mobile.

models.name: Llama 3.2 3B
models.note: Lightweight edge model for local summarization, rewriting and instruction following.

models.name: Llama 3.2 90B Vision
models.note: Multimodal flagship rivaling closed models like Claude 3 Haiku on image tasks.

models.name: Llama 3.3 70B
models.note: Text-only 70B matching Llama 3.1 405B quality at roughly 4-5x lower serving cost.

models.name: Llama 4 Maverick
models.note: Natively multimodal MoE flagship; 17B active over 400B total across 128 experts.

models.name: Llama 4 Scout
models.note: Meta's first MoE Llama; 17B active over 109B total (16 experts) with a 10M-token context window.

models.name: Turing-NLG (17B)
models.note: At launch the largest Transformer LM ever trained (17B); showcased Microsoft's DeepSpeed/ZeRO; research-only, never openly released.

models.name: Phi-1 (1.3B)
models.note: Launched the 'Textbooks Are All You Need' thesis; a 1.3B code model hitting ~50% HumanEval, proving curated synthetic data beats scale.

models.name: WizardCoder-Python-34B V1.0
models.note: Code-LLM built on Code Llama via Code Evol-Instruct; 73.2 pass@1 HumanEval, then the top open coding model (ICLR 2024).

models.name: Phi-1.5 (1.3B)
models.note: Extended Phi-1's textbook approach to common-sense reasoning and NLU; near-SOTA among sub-10B models, MIT-licensed.

models.name: Orca 2 (13B)
models.note: Reasoning-focused Llama-2 fine-tunes (7B & 13B) using 'Prompt Erasure'; rivaled models ~10x larger on reasoning tasks.

models.name: Phi-2 (2.7B)
models.note: Breakout small model that matched/beat 7B-13B models on many benchmarks; widely adopted, MIT-licensed.

models.name: WizardLM-2 8x22B
models.note: MoE (141B, ~39B active) instruct fine-tune of Mixtral 8x22B via Evol-Instruct; MS's strongest open Wizard model, near-GPT-4 in internal evals.

models.name: Phi-3-mini (3.8B)
models.note: First production-grade Phi; ~GPT-3.5-level quality that runs on a phone, with 4K/128K context variants, MIT-licensed.

models.name: Phi-3-medium (14B)
models.note: Largest dense Phi-3 (~78% MMLU), 4K/128K context; top of the Phi-3 quality-cost curve, MIT-licensed.

models.name: Phi-3-small (7B)
models.note: 7B mid-tier Phi-3 (~75% MMLU), 8K/128K context; announced with the Phi-3 report, released at Build 2024.

models.name: Phi-3.5-mini (3.8B)
models.note: Refresh of Phi-3-mini with much stronger multilingual support (20+ languages) and 128K context; beats Llama-3.1 8B on many tasks.

models.name: Phi-3.5-MoE (16x3.8B, 6.6B active)
models.note: First Phi mixture-of-experts (16 experts, 6.6B active); rivals Gemini-1.5-Flash and beats Llama-3.1 8B, MIT-licensed.

models.name: Phi-3.5-vision (4.2B)
models.note: Multimodal Phi with 4.2B params and 128K context; added multi-frame image understanding over Phi-3-vision.

models.name: Phi-4 (14B)
models.note: Flagship 14B trained on ~9.8T tokens of heavy synthetic data; surpassed teacher GPT-4o on GPQA and MATH. Weights on HF Jan 8, 2025 (MIT).

models.name: Phi-4-mini (3.8B)
models.note: Compact text-only Phi-4 (3.8B, 128K context) with built-in function calling; tops the 3-4B class, MIT-licensed.

models.name: Phi-4-multimodal (5.6B)
models.note: Unified text + vision + speech model (5.6B, 128K) built on frozen Phi-4-mini weights with LoRA adapters.

models.name: Phi-4-reasoning (14B)
models.note: 14B reasoning specialist SFT-tuned on ~1.4M prompts with o3-mini chain-of-thought; beats DeepSeek-R1-Distill-70B on reasoning. Shipped with Phi-4-reasoning-plus.

models.name: MAI-1-preview
models.note: Microsoft AI's first foundation model trained end-to-end in-house; MoE pretrained on ~15,000 H100s, debuted ~rank 13 on LMArena, feeding Copilot.

models.name: WizardLM 7B
models.note: Original WizardLM: LLaMA tuned on Evol-Instruct data (LLM-evolved complex instructions), a method that seeded WizardCoder/WizardMath and much of the field.

models.name: abab6.5
models.note: MiniMax's first trillion-parameter MoE (undisclosed exact count), 200K-token context, API-only.

models.name: MiniMax-Text-01
models.note: 456B MoE (45.9B active) using Lightning Attention; 1M-token training context, open weights.

models.name: MiniMax-VL-01
models.note: Vision-language sibling of MiniMax-Text-01 in the open-weight MiniMax-01 series.

models.name: MiniMax-M1
models.note: World's first open-weight large-scale hybrid-attention reasoning model; 1M-token native context.

models.name: MiniMax-M2
models.note: Compact 229B MoE (~10B active) tuned for coding and agentic workflows; MIT-licensed weights.

models.name: Mistral 7B
models.note: Debut open model; Apache-2.0 7B that outperformed Llama 2 13B and put Mistral on the map.

models.name: Mistral Medium (original)
models.note: Early closed top-tier endpoint launched with la Plateforme; direct ancestor of Medium 3.

models.name: Mixtral 8x7B
models.note: First strong open sparse MoE (8 experts); GPT-3.5-class quality at ~13B active params.

models.name: Mistral Large
models.note: First closed GPT-4-class flagship; launched alongside Le Chat and the Microsoft/Azure deal.

models.name: Mixtral 8x22B
models.note: Larger Apache-2.0 MoE, 141B total / ~39B active, strong multilingual, math and code.

models.name: Codestral 22B
models.note: First dedicated code model; fill-in-the-middle, 80+ languages, 32k context.

models.name: Codestral Mamba (7B)
models.note: 7B Mamba2-architecture code model with linear-time inference and long-context retrieval.

models.name: Mistral NeMo 12B
models.note: 12B dense model built with NVIDIA; 128k context, Apache 2.0, new Tekken tokenizer.

models.name: Mistral Large 2 (24.07)
models.note: 123B open-weights flagship rivaling Llama 3.1 405B on code and math (research license).

models.name: Pixtral 12B
models.note: First Mistral multimodal model; 12B vision-language, 128k context, Apache 2.0.

models.name: Ministral 3B
models.note: Edge-class 3B model (API-only, weights withheld) for on-device and low-latency use.

models.name: Ministral 8B
models.note: Open-weights 8B edge model with interleaved sliding-window attention (research license).

models.name: Mistral Large 24.11
models.note: Nov 2024 refresh of Large 2 with improved long-context, function calling and system prompt.

models.name: Pixtral Large (124B)
models.note: 124B multimodal built on Large 2; strong chart, document and math-vision reasoning.

models.name: Codestral 25.01
models.note: Faster closed Codestral refresh with improved tokenizer and more efficient architecture.

models.name: Mistral Small 3 (24B)
models.note: 24B Apache-2.0 latency-optimized model competitive with much larger open rivals.

models.name: Mistral Small 3.1 (24B)
models.note: First Mistral Small with native vision-language support; 24B, 128k context, Apache 2.0.

models.name: Mistral Medium 3
models.note: Cost-efficient closed frontier model claimed at ~90% of Claude 3.7 Sonnet across benchmarks.

models.name: Devstral Small
models.note: 24B open agentic-coding model; 46.8% SWE-bench Verified, best open model under 100B at launch.

models.name: Magistral Medium
models.note: Closed enterprise reasoning model; 73.6% AIME2024, fast chain-of-thought via Le Chat.

models.name: Magistral Small
models.note: Mistral's first reasoning model; 24B open-weights (Apache 2.0), transparent multilingual chains.

models.name: Mistral Small 3.2 (24B)
models.note: Incremental 24B update improving instruction following, repetition and function calling.

models.name: Kimi Chat
models.note: First AI assistant to support 200K-character (~128K token) lossless long context.

models.name: Kimi k1.5
models.note: Moonshot's first RL-trained multimodal reasoning model; claimed OpenAI o1-level math/coding.

models.name: Kimi K2
models.note: 1T-param MoE (32B active) agentic model, open weights under modified MIT; trained with MuonClip.

models.name: Kimi K2 Thinking
models.note: Open-weight 1T MoE reasoning agent (INT4, 256K ctx) doing 200-300 sequential tool calls.

models.name: MPT-7B
models.note: Commercially usable 7B trained from scratch on 1T tokens with ALiBi and FlashAttention, Apache 2.0.

models.name: MPT-7B-Instruct
models.note: Short-form instruction-following MPT-7B fine-tune (dolly-hhrlhf), CC-BY-SA-3.0.

models.name: MPT-7B-StoryWriter-65k+
models.note: Long-context fiction model fine-tuned at 65k tokens; extrapolates past 84k via ALiBi.

models.name: MPT-30B
models.note: Larger MPT trained on 1T tokens with an 8k context window on H100s, surpassing original GPT-3 quality, Apache 2.0.

models.name: MPT-30B-Instruct
models.note: Instruction-tuned MPT-30B for long-context single-turn tasks, CC-BY-SA-3.0.

models.name: NAVER HyperCLOVA X
models.note: NAVER's sovereign Korean-first LLM (~204B), successor to 2021's HyperCLOVA; API/service only.

models.name: NAVER HyperCLOVA X SEED Think 32B
models.note: Open-weights multimodal reasoning model; one of the strongest Korean open models on the Artificial Analysis index, topping EXAONE 4.0 32B.

models.name: OpenHermes 2.5 Mistral 7B
models.note: Hugely influential open Mistral fine-tune; the OpenHermes dataset seeded a wave of 7B assistant models.

models.name: Nous Hermes 2 Mixtral 8x7B DPO
models.note: Flagship MoE fine-tune of Mixtral 8x7B, DPO-aligned on 1M+ mostly GPT-4-generated samples.

models.name: Hermes 3 405B
models.note: First full-parameter fine-tune of Llama 3.1 405B; Nous's flagship neutrally-aligned generalist, released in 8B/70B/405B.

models.name: Megatron-Turing NLG 530B
models.note: Joint NVIDIA-Microsoft dense transformer; largest monolithic LLM at launch (530B), a scaling landmark, weights never publicly released.

models.name: Nemotron-3 8B
models.note: NVIDIA's first Nemotron-branded open LLM family, aimed at enterprise chat and copilot builds via the NeMo framework.

models.name: Nemotron-4 15B
models.note: 15B multilingual base trained on 8T tokens (50+ languages, 40+ code langs); beat 4x-larger models on multilingual tasks.

models.name: Nemotron-4 340B
models.note: Open 340B Base/Instruct/Reward family under NVIDIA Open Model License; pioneered large-scale synthetic-data generation (98%+ synthetic alignment).

models.name: Llama-3.1-Minitron 4B
models.note: 4B model pruned and distilled from Llama-3.1-8B (width/depth); showcased NVIDIA's compression recipe with 40x fewer training tokens.

models.name: Llama-3.1-Nemotron-70B Instruct
models.note: RLHF-tuned (REINFORCE) Llama-3.1-70B that topped Arena Hard (85.0), AlpacaEval and MT-Bench, edging GPT-4o and Claude 3.5 Sonnet.

models.name: Llama-3.3-Nemotron-Super-49B v1
models.note: NAS-compressed Llama-3.3-70B reasoning model with a toggleable reasoning mode; fits a single H100/H200 at high throughput.

models.name: Nemotron-H 56B
models.note: Hybrid Mamba-Transformer family (8B/47B/56B) replacing most attention with Mamba-2 layers for up to 3x faster inference at comparable accuracy.

models.name: Llama-3.1-Nemotron-Ultra-253B v1
models.note: Flagship open reasoning model derived from Llama-3.1-405B; briefly the most intelligent open model, outperforming DeepSeek-R1 at half the size.

models.name: Nemotron Nano 2 (Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2)
models.note: Hybrid Mamba-Transformer reasoning model (9B, pruned from a 12B/20T-token base) with up to 6x higher throughput than Qwen3-8B in reasoning settings.

models.name: Nemotron 3 Ultra 550B A55B
models.note: NVIDIA's frontier open MoE (550B total / 55B active); hybrid LatentMoE + Mamba-2 with 1M-token context, flagship of the Nemotron 3 family.

models.name: Mistral-NeMo 12B
models.note: Mistral + NVIDIA collaboration; 12B with 128K context, Apache-2.0 weights, quantization-aware for single-GPU FP8 inference.

models.name: Krutrim-2 (12B)
models.note: 12B Indic LLM built on Mistral-NeMo; 128K context, English plus 22 Indian languages.

models.name: GPT-3 175B
models.note: 175B few-shot learner that put LLMs on the map and seeded the OpenAI API.

models.name: OpenAI Codex
models.note: GPT-3 fine-tuned on code; powered early GitHub Copilot and introduced the HumanEval benchmark.

models.name: GPT-3.5 Turbo
models.note: Chat-tuned model behind ChatGPT's launch; cheap, fast, and mass-adopted.

models.name: GPT-4
models.note: First GPT-4; multimodal image+text input, 8K/32K context, large reasoning jump.

models.name: GPT-4 Turbo
models.note: 128K-context, cheaper GPT-4 with knowledge to Apr 2023; announced at the first DevDay.

models.name: GPT-4o
models.note: Natively multimodal 'omni' flagship (text/audio/vision), faster and cheaper than GPT-4 Turbo.

models.name: GPT-4o mini
models.note: Small, cheap GPT-4o sibling that replaced GPT-3.5 Turbo as the default budget model.

models.name: OpenAI o1-mini
models.note: Cost-efficient reasoning model tuned for STEM and coding.

models.name: OpenAI o1-preview
models.note: First public reasoning model; think-before-answer chain-of-thought that launched the o-series.

models.name: OpenAI o1
models.note: Full reasoning flagship; strong math/science/coding via hidden reasoning tokens.

models.name: OpenAI o3-mini
models.note: Cost-efficient reasoning model with selectable effort; shipped amid the DeepSeek R1 wave.

models.name: GPT-4.5
models.note: 'Orion' — OpenAI's largest non-reasoning model, shipped as a research preview and later deprecated.

models.name: GPT-4.1
models.note: API-only coding-focused flagship with 1M-token context and June 2024 knowledge cutoff.

models.name: GPT-4.1 mini
models.note: Balanced mid-tier of the 4.1 family with 1M-token context and vision.

models.name: GPT-4.1 nano
models.note: Fastest, cheapest 4.1 model; 1M-token context.

models.name: OpenAI o3
models.note: Flagship reasoning model with tool use; major gains on math, coding, and ARC-AGI.

models.name: OpenAI o4-mini
models.note: Compact reasoning model with strong price/performance and native tool use.

models.name: OpenAI o3-pro
models.note: Higher-compute o3 variant for the most reliable answers; replaced o1-pro.

models.name: gpt-oss-120b
models.note: OpenAI's first open-weight model since GPT-2; 117B MoE (5.1B active), Apache 2.0.

models.name: gpt-oss-20b
models.note: 21B MoE (3.6B active) open-weight sibling that runs on consumer hardware; Apache 2.0.

models.name: GPT-5
models.note: Unified model merging o-series reasoning with fast GPT responses via an auto-router.

models.name: GPT-5 mini
models.note: Cost-efficient mid-tier of the GPT-5 family.

models.name: GPT-5 nano
models.note: Smallest, fastest GPT-5 variant for high-volume, latency-sensitive workloads.

models.name: GPT-5-Codex
models.note: GPT-5 optimized for agentic coding; can run autonomously for hours inside Codex.

models.name: GPT-5.1
models.note: Warmer, more conversational refresh with adaptive reasoning; Instant and Thinking modes.

models.name: GPT-5.2
models.note: Flagship refresh adding a Pro reasoning tier with stronger tool use and long-context.

models.name: GPT-5.5
models.note: Flagship (codename 'Spud') with Thinking and Pro tiers; API access gated at launch.

models.name: GPT-5.6
models.note: Current flagship with Luna/Terra/Sol tiers; Sol billed as OpenAI's best coding model yet.

models.name: MiniCPM-2B
models.note: Pioneering edge LLM (~2.4B) matching Mistral-7B on many benchmarks; runs on-device.

models.name: MiniCPM-Llama3-V 2.5
models.note: 8B phone-sized MLLM that first reached GPT-4V-level, surpassing Gemini Pro and Claude 3 on OpenCompass.

models.name: MiniCPM-V 2.6
models.note: 8B MLLM (SigLip-400M + Qwen2-7B) adding multi-image and video understanding on-device.

models.name: MiniCPM3-4B
models.note: Third-gen 4B on-device LLM that outperforms GPT-3.5-Turbo with function-calling and code interpreter.

models.name: MiniCPM-o 2.6
models.note: 8B any-to-any omni model (vision+speech+language) with real-time streaming, GPT-4o-level on edge.

models.name: Teuken-7B (instruct)
models.note: EU-funded model trained on all 24 official EU languages; Apache-2.0 commercial variant.

models.name: InternVL2.5-78B
models.note: First open-source MLLM to exceed 70% on MMMU (InternViT-6B + Qwen2.5-72B), rivaling GPT-4o.

models.name: InternVL3-78B
models.note: Flagship of the natively-multimodal InternVL3 series; adds tool use, GUI agents and 3D perception.

models.name: OpenLLaMA 7B
models.note: Apache-2.0 from-scratch reproduction of Meta LLaMA on RedPajama (3B/7B/13B, plus v2); a permissive, drop-in base free of LLaMA's license.

models.name: Sonar
models.note: Perplexity's in-house web-grounded answer model (built on Llama 3.3 70B), served on Cerebras at ~1200 tok/s.

models.name: R1-1776
models.note: Open (MIT) post-train of DeepSeek-R1 that removes CCP censorship while preserving reasoning quality.

models.name: PLaMo-100B
models.note: Ground-up 100B Japanese-proficiency model from Preferred Elements; basis for the later PLaMo Prime line.

models.name: Reka Core
models.note: Reka's frontier multimodal model (text/image/video/audio), approaching GPT-4V-class on multimodal benchmarks.

models.name: Reka Edge (7B)
models.note: 7B compact multimodal model for on-device/local use; outperformed Gemma 7B and Mistral 7B in Reka's report.

models.name: Reka Flash (21B)
models.note: 21B mid-size multimodal model balancing capability and efficiency in the Core/Flash/Edge series.

models.name: Reka Flash 3
models.note: 21B open (Apache 2.0) reasoning model trained from scratch with 'budget forcing'; rivals o1-mini class.

models.name: rinna japanese-gpt-neox-3.6b
models.note: One of Japan's earliest openly released, MIT-licensed instruction-tuned Japanese LLMs.

models.name: CodeGen 16B (Mono)
models.note: Early open program-synthesis LLM (350M-16B) trained on TPU-v4; the Mono variant was competitive with OpenAI Codex on HumanEval.

models.name: CodeT5+ 16B
models.note: Encoder-decoder code LLM family (220M-16B) with flexible modules for code understanding and generation; InstructCodeT5+ tuned variant.

models.name: XGen-7B 8K
models.note: General-purpose 7B LLM trained on 1.5T tokens with an 8K context window under Apache 2.0; Salesforce's long-context entry.

models.name: CodeGen2.5 7B
models.note: 'Small but mighty' 7B code model rivaling 15B-16B models (StarCoder-15B, CodeGen1/2-16B) with infill and Flash-attention serving.

models.name: Sarvam-1 (2B)
models.note: India's first from-scratch open foundation model; 2B optimized for 10 Indic languages.

models.name: Sarvam-105B
models.note: India's first sovereign 100B+ open model; Apache-2.0 MoE (~9B active), 128K context, 22 languages.

models.name: Sarashina2-70B
models.note: SoftBank subsidiary's MIT-licensed Japanese base model, trained ground-up on ~2.1T tokens.

models.name: GigaChat
models.note: Sberbank's Russian ChatGPT rival built on the NeONKA ensemble; API/app only.

models.name: Poro 34B
models.note: Fully open Apache-2.0 Finnish/English/code model; pivotal European low-resource open LLM.

models.name: Viking 7B
models.note: First open LLM covering all Nordic languages; part of the 7B/13B/33B Viking family.

models.name: Snowflake Arctic
models.note: Apache-2.0 dense+MoE hybrid (480B total / 17B active, 128 experts); enterprise-focused, training-efficient design.

models.name: StableLM-Alpha 7B
models.note: Stability AI's debut LLM suite (3B & 7B alpha); an early open text model in the post-LLaMA open-weights wave.

models.name: StableLM-3B-4e1t
models.note: Well-regarded 3B base trained for ~4 epochs over 1T tokens; a strong small-model baseline of its era.

models.name: StableLM Zephyr 3B
models.note: DPO-tuned chat model built on StableLM-3B-4e1t; aimed at capable assistants on edge devices.

models.name: Stable LM 2 1.6B
models.note: Compact multilingual (7-language) model trained on 2T tokens; small-footprint member of the StableLM 2 line.

models.name: Stable LM 2 12B
models.note: Mid-size multilingual flagship of StableLM 2; instruct variant tuned for tool use and function calling.

models.name: Stable Beluga 1 (FreeWilly 1) 65B
models.note: The original LLaMA 65B Orca-method sibling released alongside Beluga 2; strong open-access reasoning model of mid-2023.

models.name: Stable Beluga 2 (FreeWilly 2) 70B
models.note: Llama 2 70B fine-tuned with Orca-style synthetic data; briefly topped the Open LLM Leaderboard at #1 in July 2023.

models.name: Alpaca 7B
models.note: LLaMA 7B fine-tuned on 52K self-instruct demos for ~$600; proved cheap synthetic instruction-tuning works and sparked the open fine-tune wave.

models.name: Step-1
models.note: StepFun's debut 100B+ bilingual LLM, released via API at company launch.

models.name: Step-2
models.note: First trillion-parameter MoE from a Chinese firm; Step-2-16k later ranked ~5th on LiveBench.

models.name: Step-1V
models.note: StepFun's first multimodal (vision) model, adding image understanding on the Step-1 base.

models.name: Step-3
models.note: Open-source 321B MoE (38B active) multimodal reasoning VLM under Apache-2.0.

models.name: Hunyuan (1st-gen)
models.note: Tencent's first in-house foundation model (100B+ params), launched for enterprises on Tencent Cloud.

models.name: Hunyuan-Large (A52B)
models.note: 389B total / 52B-active MoE; among the largest open-weights models at release, rivaling Llama-3.1-405B.

models.name: Hunyuan-TurboS
models.note: First ultra-large Hybrid Transformer-Mamba MoE (560B total / 56B active) fast-thinking base model.

models.name: Hunyuan-T1
models.note: Mamba-powered deep-reasoning flagship built on TurboS, benchmarked against DeepSeek R1 and o1.

models.name: Hunyuan-A13B
models.note: Efficient open MoE (80B total / 13B active) with dual fast/slow reasoning and 256K context.

models.name: Falcon-40B
models.note: Topped the Hugging Face Open LLM Leaderboard in mid-2023; first UAE-built model to lead open-source rankings.

models.name: Falcon-7B
models.note: Apache-2.0 causal decoder trained on RefinedWeb; the small, widely-adopted entry point of the original Falcon line.

models.name: Falcon-180B
models.note: Billed as the world's most powerful openly released LLM at launch; trained on 3.5T tokens.

models.name: Falcon 2 11B
models.note: Second-gen Falcon trained on 5.5T tokens; multilingual with a vision-to-language variant, positioned against Llama 3 8B.

models.name: Falcon Mamba 7B
models.note: First competitive attention-free State-Space (Mamba) open model; constant memory over sequence length.

models.name: Falcon3-10B
models.note: Flagship of Falcon 3, depth-upscaled from the 7B; topped Hugging Face's sub-13B leaderboard at release.

models.name: Falcon3-7B
models.note: From-scratch 14T-token anchor of the Falcon 3 family with 32K context; strong math/code for its size.

models.name: Falcon-H1 34B
models.note: Flagship of the hybrid Transformer+SSM Falcon-H1 series; up to 256K context, rivals ~70B dense models.

models.name: Falcon-H1R 7B
models.note: Reasoning-tuned hybrid model with test-time scaling; matches reasoning models 2-7x its size.

models.name: RedPajama-INCITE-Base-3B-v1
models.note: First RedPajama model; strongest open 3B of its class, small enough to run on a single consumer GPU.

models.name: RedPajama-INCITE-7B-Base
models.note: Fully reproducible 7B trained on 1T tokens of the open RedPajama-1T dataset with released checkpoints, Apache 2.0.

models.name: RedPajama-INCITE-7B-Chat
models.note: Chat variant fine-tuned on OASST1 and Dolly v2 via OpenChatKit, Apache 2.0.

models.name: RedPajama-INCITE-7B-Instruct
models.note: Instruction-tuned 7B that topped open 7B models on HELM few-shot benchmarks at release.

models.name: Koala 13B
models.note: LLaMA fine-tuned on curated web dialogue (ShareGPT + HC3); a parallel effort to Vicuna often preferred over Alpaca in human evals.

models.name: Starling-7B (alpha)
models.note: RLAIF fine-tune of OpenChat/Mistral using the GPT-4-labeled Nectar dataset; scored 8.09 MT-Bench, then the best 7B and behind only GPT-4.

models.name: Gorilla 7B
models.note: LLaMA fine-tuned for accurate API/function calls over 1600+ APIs (APIBench); an early tool-use specialist that beat GPT-4 at writing API calls.

models.name: Guanaco 33B
models.note: The single-24GB-GPU QLoRA showcase size; demonstrated that a 33B chatbot could be finetuned on consumer hardware in ~12 hours.

models.name: Guanaco 65B
models.note: Flagship of the QLoRA paper: 4-bit LoRA fine-tune of LLaMA 65B on OASST1 reaching ~99% of ChatGPT on the Vicuna benchmark in 24 GPU-hours.

models.name: Upstage SOLAR 10.7B
models.note: Depth-Up-Scaling model that briefly topped the Hugging Face Open LLM Leaderboard; put Korea's Upstage on the map.

models.name: Upstage Solar Pro
models.note: 22B single-GPU flagship; the Sept 2024 preview topped the <70B Open LLM Leaderboard.

models.name: Upstage Solar Pro 2
models.note: Billed as Korea's first 'frontier' model; hybrid chat/reasoning modes, rivals GPT-4.1-class systems at 31B.

models.name: Palmyra Med 70B
models.note: Open-weights healthcare specialist scoring ~86% average across medical benchmarks, released alongside Palmyra Fin.

models.name: Palmyra X 004
models.note: Writer's enterprise frontier model (~150B); topped Berkeley's function-calling leaderboard at launch.

models.name: Palmyra X5
models.note: Adaptive-reasoning enterprise LLM with a 1M-token context window, trained with synthetic data for ~$1M in GPUs.

models.name: Grok-1
models.note: xAI's first LLM; a 314B-parameter Mixture-of-Experts base model, open-sourced under Apache 2.0 in March 2024.

models.name: Grok-1.5
models.note: First major closed iteration; 128K-token context with stronger math and reasoning, powering the X chatbot.

models.name: Grok-2
models.note: Frontier flagship with image generation; competitive on the LMSYS Chatbot Arena at launch.

models.name: Grok-2 mini
models.note: Smaller, faster, cost-efficient sibling of Grok-2, launched alongside it in beta.

models.name: Grok-3
models.note: Reasoning-focused flagship trained on the Colossus supercluster (~10x Grok-2 compute); added Think/Big Brain modes.

models.name: Grok-3 mini
models.note: Cost-efficient reasoning model; strong math and coding performance at a lower price point.

models.name: Grok 4 Heavy
models.note: Multi-agent 'heavy' configuration of Grok-4 for maximum reasoning, offered on the SuperGrok Heavy tier.

models.name: Grok-4
models.note: Frontier reasoning flagship with native tool use and top benchmark scores (incl. ARC-AGI) at launch.

models.name: YandexGPT 5 Pro
models.note: Russia's flagship closed LLM powering Alice; claimed parity with GPT-4o on Russian tasks.

models.name: GLM-130B
models.note: GPT-3-scale open bilingual (EN/ZH) 130B dense model; released code, weights and training logs (ICLR 2023).

models.name: CodeGeeX (13B)
models.note: Multilingual code-generation model covering 23 programming languages; introduced the HumanEval-X benchmark (KDD 2023).

models.name: ChatGLM-6B
models.note: Pivotal open bilingual dialogue model runnable on consumer GPUs (INT4/6GB); China's early open ChatGPT-style release.

models.name: ChatGLM2-6B
models.note: Second-gen 6B chat model with longer 32K context, stronger benchmarks and faster inference.

models.name: CogVLM-17B
models.note: Open visual-language model (10B vision + 7B language) with SOTA-level results on 10 cross-modal benchmarks.

models.name: ChatGLM3-6B
models.note: Third-gen 6B model adding native function calling, code interpreter and agent tool use.

models.name: GLM-4
models.note: Fourth-gen flagship unveiled at Zhipu DevDay; 128K context and 'All Tools' agent, positioned against GPT-4.

models.name: GLM-4-9B-Chat
models.note: Open 9B GLM-4 checkpoint (up to 128K/1M context) beating Llama-3-8B across semantics, math, code and reasoning.

models.name: GLM-4V-9B
models.note: Open multimodal GLM-4 variant adding 1120x1120 high-res image understanding to the 9B language backbone.

models.name: GLM-4-Plus
models.note: Upgraded closed flagship unveiled at KDD 2024, claimed on par with GPT-4o in language, instruction-following and long text.

models.name: GLM-4-32B-0414
models.note: MIT-licensed open 32B base pre-trained on 15T tokens; competitive with GPT-4o / DeepSeek-V3 at a fraction of the size.

models.name: GLM-Z1-32B-0414
models.note: Open reasoning model built on GLM-4-32B via cold-start + RL; strong math/logic, marketed as much faster than DeepSeek-R1.

models.name: GLM-4.5 (355B-A32B)
models.note: MIT-licensed MoE flagship (355B total / 32B active) for agentic, reasoning and coding, with thinking/non-thinking modes.

models.name: GLM-4.5-Air (106B-A12B)
models.note: Lighter open MoE sibling of GLM-4.5 (106B total / 12B active) targeting efficient agentic deployment under MIT license.
